task.title: Reject invalid stored stream versions during append
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/390 by enforcing the positive stream-version invariant when append reads the current state of a corrupted or externally modified SQLite stream.
acceptance_criteria: Appending to a stream whose maximum stored version is zero returns EventLogError::InvalidStoredVersion before expected-version matching.
The invalid stored-version error carries the authoritative value, has a stable diagnostic, and exposes no underlying source.
The rejected append writes no row and valid optimistic-concurrency behavior remains unchanged.
The public documentation describes the append-side persisted-data failure.
The pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused integration test that inserts stream version zero by explicitly bypassing SQLite check constraints, then attempts an append with Exact(0).
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed to compile because EventLogError had no InvalidStoredVersion variant, exposing that append had no stable classification for invalid persisted current versions.

attempts.summary: Validated the stored maximum stream version at the existing signed-to-public conversion boundary.
attempts.patch: stored_version_to_u64 now maps negative values and zero to EventLogError::InvalidStoredVersion before append performs expected-version matching.

attempts.summary: Verified the stable error contract and absence of a partial write while keeping the implementation localized.
attempts.patch: The regression test asserts the exact variant, display text, absent source, and unchanged one-row count; documentation records the append-side failure.

attempts.summary: Completed independent review and monitored the required pull-request checks through merge.
attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit completed with no actionable comments, Rust quality gate run 29317745671 passed, and pull request 391 squash-merged as a092849.

outcome.summary: Append now rejects an invalid persisted current stream version before optimistic-concurrency matching, reports the authoritative value as a stable data error, and writes nothing.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_zero_as_the_current_stored_version_without_writing -- --exact
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29317745671 for pull request 391
CodeRabbit review for pull request 391: review completed with no actionable comments and required status passed
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0017-invalid-current-stream-version.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 17 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Apply persisted-data invariants consistently at every read boundary: replay validation alone does not protect append logic that also reads stream metadata.
Validate the stored current version before optimistic-concurrency matching so an invalid value cannot be legitimized by a matching caller expectation.
Keep corruption distinct from storage access failure by preserving the authoritative SQLite integer in a dedicated domain error.
Assert the persisted row count after rejection to prove a failed concurrency-path append did not partially mutate the stream.
